article_title: GitHub Will Train AI Models on Copilot User Interactions by Default Starting April 24, Sparking Developer Backlash

content_preview.title: GitHub Will Train AI Models on Copilot User Interactions by Default Starting April 24, Sparking Developer Backlash
content_preview.summary: GitHub's new opt-out policy lets it use code snippets, prompts, and context from Free, Pro, and Pro+ users to train AI models, drawing sharp criticism from developers who call it a bait-and-switch.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

GitHub announced on March 25 that it will begin using interaction data from Copilot Free, Pro, and Pro+ users to train and improve its AI models, effective April 24, 2026. The policy is opt-out by default, meaning developers must actively disable the setting to prevent their code snippets, prompts, and surrounding context from feeding into model training. Copilot Business and Enterprise customers are excluded, as are students and teachers enrolled in GitHub Education, according to t...

editor_notes.source_verification: The Register confirmed April 24 default, opt-out, private repos via Copilot, 59 thumbs-down, data types.
